mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that supports corporate earnings revisions and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in over 65% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, prompting institutional deleveraging and negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines fail to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases, with a positive return frequency exceeding 58%.

mechanism_narrative: Lower month-over-month changes in the consumer price index reduce inflation expectations and real interest rate pressures, allowing equity valuations to expand and generating positive weekly forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over consecutive 1-year windows or CPI declines coincide with negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between stagnant nonfarm payrolls growth and rising unemployment indicates labor market deterioration that suppresses consumer spending forecasts and forces negative quarterly equity repricing.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 60-day returns in over 55% of triggered instances or interaction coefficient flips sign in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Real gross domestic product growth exceeding 2.5 percent annually confirms robust economic momentum that lifts corporate profit margins and generates positive monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output that improves supply chain efficiency and supports positive short-term equity market performance.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production changes fail to predict positive 5-day returns, with a negative return frequency exceeding 55% across rolling 2-year samples.
